what_it_does: This file is a Node.js CLI entry point for the 'episodic-memory stats' command. It parses process.argv, prints a help message if requested, otherwise calls getIndexStats() and prints the result formatted by formatStats().
observations: Prints a usage/help block and exits 0 when help is requested
On success, logs the formatted stats to stdout
On error, logs the error to stderr and exits with code 1
Contains no other subcommands or options
standouts: Very thin CLI wrapper that delegates all statistics logic to stats.js
Includes a detailed help/usage message for a single command
conventions: Uses process.argv.slice(2) for raw CLI argument access
Uses async promise chaining (.then/.catch) rather than async/await
Uses console.log for output and console.error for errors
Uses process.exit for explicit success/failure codes
Imports a sibling module with a .js extension in a TypeScript file
concerns: Does not validate or warn on unknown/ignored arguments
notes: A minimal CLI wrapper that either prints help or fetches and displays formatted archive statistics by delegating to stats.js.
questions: What is the exact shape of the stats object returned by getIndexStats?
How is the 'episodic-memory stats' command registered/installed?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose (display statistics about the indexed conversation archive) matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Separation of CLI presentation from statistics logic
intention_signals: Provide a simple, discoverable command-line way for users to view summary statistics about an episodic-memory conversation archive.
contribution: Provides the CLI invocation path, help text, and error-handling shell around the stats functionality, keeping presentation separate from computation.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js CLI entry point that prints usage help or retrieves and displays formatted index statistics for an episodic-memory conversation archive.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a simple command-line way to view archive statistics.
